The RTL Arabic + Supabase + Next.js 14 admin shell pattern built in DEV-350 is a 3-times-rule extraction candidate per QA Gate 3 verdict.

## Pattern to extract

A reusable scaffold for Arabic RTL admin dashboards using:
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS (logical properties for RTL)
- Supabase SSR client (`createBrowserClient`) + `proxy.ts` auth guard
- Role-based route guards checking `app_metadata.role` from JWT
- Custom UI primitives (`Card`, `Badge`, `Button`, `Input`, `Spinner`, `Sidebar`) without Radix UI
- Loading (`animate-pulse` skeleton), empty (Arabic icon + copy), error (red banner) state pattern
- Zod form validation wired into CRUD modal `handleSave()`
- Kuwait timezone (`Intl.DateTimeFormat` with `Asia/Kuwait`)
- Arabic empty-state copy convention

## Task

Run `reusable-template-extractor` skill on `attendance-admin/` (session `118782a7`) to extract this pattern as a reusable skill/template.

Output should include:
- Skill definition (purpose, trigger, inputs, steps, output, quality checklist)
- Template scaffold files
- Notes on what is app-specific vs. generic

## Why this matters

This pattern (RTL Arabic Supabase Next.js admin shell) will be used in multiple Kuwaiti business apps. Extracting it now prevents re-implementing from scratch each time and ensures RTL, auth, and security patterns are consistent across the workspace.
issues.title: SKILL: Extract RTL Arabic + Supabase + Next.js admin shell as reusable template

The `attendance-admin/` Next.js web frontend was built and QA-approved in Multica session `118782a7` (local filesystem). Before production deployment, the code must be committed to a GitHub repository.

## Options

1. **Dedicated repo** — create `newtechkw/attendance-admin` and push `attendance-admin/` as the root
2. **Subdirectory in existing repo** — commit `attendance-admin/` as `/web` subdirectory of `adminnewtech/attendance-app` (mobile app + web in monorepo)

Recommendation: option 2 (monorepo) keeps mobile + web together since they share Supabase project, schema, and deployment secrets.

## Steps

1. Check out the session `118782a7` working directory and verify the `attendance-admin/` build is clean (`npm run build` passes).
2. Commit `attendance-admin/` to the chosen repo/path.
3. Set up `.env.local` equivalent as environment variables in Vercel (or chosen host).
4. Pin `pr_url` (or `repo_url`) to DEV-350 metadata once committed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `attendance-admin/` source is committed and pushed to GitHub
- [ ] CI passes (TypeScript check + lint)
- [ ] Vercel (or hosting) deployment configured with Supabase env vars
- [ ] `pr_url` or `deploy_url` pinned to [DEV-350](mention://issue/311411b9-4454-49ab-b863-fcabc2efadfc) metadata

## Dependencies

- DEV-364 (RLS policies) — must be applied to production Supabase before exposing to real users
- DEV-363 (Rate limiting) — must be configured at deployment layer before production launch
- Admin provisioning of `newtechkw/attendance-app` repo access (if monorepo path chosen)

## Risk

Without committing to a repo, the frontend exists only in a Multica runtime session and will be lost when the session is recycled.

issues.description:
Before the Android Attendance admin web app goes to production, all user-scoped Supabase tables must have Row-Level Security (RLS) policies in place and verified.

**Tables requiring RLS:**
- `attendance_logs`
- `employees`
- `offices`
- `shifts`
- `leave_requests`
- `tenant_settings`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RLS enabled on every table above
- [ ] Policy logic: a user can only read/write rows scoped to their `tenant_id` (multi-tenant isolation)
- [ ] Super Admin can read all rows in their tenant; HR Manager scoped to their department; Dept Manager scoped to their team
- [ ] Verified by running queries as two test users in different tenants and confirming no cross-tenant data leakage
- [ ] RLS policy SQL documented in schema migration file

## Risk

Failure to implement RLS allows a compromised JWT to read any tenant's data via the Supabase JS client. This is a production-blocking security gate per workspace security rules.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] RLS policies implemented and migration applied to staging DB
- [ ] Cross-tenant isolation test passes (two test tenants, no row bleed)
- [ ] `security-review-checklist` item #4 (RLS) verified PASS
- [ ] QA sign-off comment on this issue before closing
issues.title: SECURITY: RLS policies on all user-scoped tables — Android Attendance

issues.description:
Before the Android Attendance admin web app goes to production, rate limiting must be implemented on `/login` and all write endpoints to prevent credential brute-force and abuse.

## Scope

- `/login` — limit to 5 failed attempts per IP per 15 minutes
- API write paths — limit POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE requests per authenticated user per minute
- Report download endpoints — limit to prevent DoS via expensive Supabase function calls

## Acceptable Implementation Options

1. **Vercel Edge Middleware** — add a rate-limit check in `proxy.ts` using an in-memory or KV store (e.g. Vercel KV / Upstash Redis)
2. **Supabase level** — use Supabase rate limiting on Auth endpoints (already built-in for `/auth/v1/token`)
3. **Cloudflare WAF** — if deployed behind Cloudflare, configure rate-limit rules at the edge

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/login` rejects requests after threshold with HTTP 429 + retry-after header
- [ ] Rate limit strategy documented (which layer, threshold values)
- [ ] No legitimate admin user would be locked out under normal usage patterns
- [ ] Tested: 6 rapid login attempts from same IP returns 429 on the 6th

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Rate limiting implemented and tested
- [ ] `security-review-checklist` item #10 (rate limiting) verified PASS
- [ ] QA sign-off comment on this issue before closing
issues.title: SECURITY: Rate limiting on /login and write endpoints — Android Attendance

issues.description:
# ADR-004 — i18n/l10n Library Standard for Arabic (Kuwaiti) + English Apps

**Status:** Accepted  
**Date:** 2026-07-01  
**Decider:** Solution Architect (solution-architect agent)  
**Project:** 04 - Architecture Database API  

---

## Context

The devosnt App Factory builds bilingual apps targeting Arabic Kuwaiti dialect + English for business and customer-facing surfaces. The workspace Language Rules mandate:

- **Internal/dev output:** English
- **Customer/marketing output:** Arabic Kuwaiti style (RTL layout)

In May–June 2026 two critical CVEs landed in the most widely deployed i18next ecosystem packages:

| CVE | Package | CVSS | Description | Fixed |
|-----|---------|------|-------------|-------|
| GHSA-2933-q333-qg83 | `i18next-fs-backend` ≤ 2.6.5 | 9.1 Critical | Prototype pollution via crafted missing-key strings | 2.6.6 |
| GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w | `i18next-http-middleware` < 3.9.7 | 9.1 Critical | Prototype pollution via dotted-key bypass in MissingKeyHandler | 3.9.7 |

The attack surface is `saveMissing: true` with untrusted user input reaching the missing-key handler — a common pattern in development environments that often leaks to staging.

Without a documented library choice, future build agents may silently adopt `next-i18next` or `i18next` without version pinning, exposing every app to these CVEs.

---

## Decision

**Two-library approach — web and mobile use separate but compatible libraries that share translation JSON files.**

| Layer | Library | Version to pin |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| Web (Next.js App Router) | `next-intl` | `^4.13.1` |
| Mobile (React Native / Expo managed) | `react-i18next` + `expo-localization` | `react-i18next ^16.0.0`, `expo-localization ^55.0.0` |

Translation files live in a shared `packages/translations/` directory (monorepo) or `public/locales/` (single-app). Both libraries consume the same flat JSON namespace files.

---

## Alternatives Considered

### Option A: `next-i18next` (web) — REJECTED

- ~450 K weekly downloads (declining).
- App Router support added in v16 (March 2026) — immature, limited community testing.
- **Dependency tree includes both `i18next-fs-backend` and `i18next-http-middleware`** — the exact packages carrying the two critical CVEs. Adopting this library forces every app to carry CVE exposure unless patch versions are pinned precisely and monitored continuously.
- RTL support is indirect: inherits from i18next's `dir()` helper, no built-in Next.js middleware integration.

### Option B: `react-intl` / FormatJS as web primary — REJECTED

- 20 KB gzipped (5× next-intl's ~4 KB).
- Requires wrapping the entire tree in a `<IntlProvider>` Client Component, creating a hard boundary that forces RSC pages to the client unnecessarily.
- No built-in Next.js routing middleware or locale detection.
- No CVEs, but ergonomically poor for App Router.

### Option C: `i18next` directly — REJECTED

- Exposes all CVEs listed above.
- Requires manual middleware wiring for Next.js App Router.

### Option D: `next-intl` web + `i18n-js` mobile

- `i18n-js` is lightweight but lacks active Expo-specific bindings and `isRTL` propagation without `react-native-localize` (which requires ejecting from Expo managed workflow).
- Rejected in favor of the Expo-endorsed `react-i18next` + `expo-localization` stack.

---

## RTL / Arabic Considerations

**Web (`next-intl`):**
- `getLocaleDirection('ar-KW')` returns `'rtl'` — set `<html dir={dir} lang={locale}>` in `app/layout.tsx`.
- Use `useLocaleDirection()` in Client Components where direction-aware rendering is needed.
- CSS: use logical properties (`margin-inline-start`, `padding-inline-end`, etc.) instead of physical `left`/`right` throughout Tailwind config (`rtl:` prefix or `@layer`).

**Mobile (`expo-localization` + React Native):**
- `expo-localization` exposes `Localization.isRTL` at runtime.
- Call `I18nManager.forceRTL(Localization.isRTL)` once in `App.tsx` before the root render.
- Use `StyleSheet` with `start`/`end` instead of `left`/`right` for layout.

**Translation file structure:**
```
locales/
  en/
    common.json
    auth.json
  ar/          # ar-KW — use ISO 639-1 "ar" with Kuwaiti dialect strings
    common.json
    auth.json
```

Arabic Kuwaiti dialect content (marketing, customer-facing) must be reviewed by a native speaker before production release. The i18n pipeline does not substitute for localization review.

---

## CVE-Safe Version Pinning Policy

1. **Do not install `i18next-fs-backend` or `i18next-http-middleware`** in any factory app. If a future dependency pulls them in transitively, audit and override immediately.
2. Pin `next-intl` to a concrete minor (`^4.13.1`); track releases via Dependabot or Renovate with a maximum 7-day merge window for patch releases.
3. Pin `expo-localization` to a concrete minor aligned with the Expo SDK version in use.
4. Run `npm audit --audit-level=high` as a required CI step. The pipeline **must fail** on high/critical CVEs with no suppression.
5. Register `@devosnt/i18n-policy` in the workspace skill library so the QA agent can verify compliance automatically.

---

## Build Graph Impact

The i18n decision affects:
- **Schema:** no direct schema impact (all translations are file-based).
- **API:** locale detection middleware added to Next.js middleware chain (no API route changes).
- **Frontend shell:** `next-intl` Provider setup in `app/[locale]/layout.tsx`; locale negotiation middleware at `middleware.ts`.
- **Mobile:** `expo-localization` setup in `App.tsx` + RTL bootstrap.
- **Auth:** login/signup pages need locale-aware redirects (handled by next-intl middleware).

---

## Reversal Conditions

This decision should be revisited if **any** of the following occur:

1. **next-intl receives a critical CVE** not patched within 48 hours of disclosure.
2. **Vercel ships a competing first-party i18n solution** with tighter App Router integration.
3. **A factory app requires pluralization rules** beyond the CLDR categories supported by next-intl's built-in engine (e.g., dual forms in Arabic — `none / one / two / few / many / other`). Current: next-intl v4 supports all six CLDR plural categories including Arabic's `few` and `many`.
4. **Expo drops support** for `expo-localization` in a future SDK in favor of a different primitive — at that point evaluate `@formatjs/intl` for mobile.
5. **Three or more factory apps** encounter shared translation infra pain (collision, namespace drift) that a monorepo i18n toolchain (e.g., Lingo.dev, Crowdin native SDK) would solve better.

---

## Acceptance Criteria (DoD for this ADR)

- [x] Top 3 libraries evaluated against CVE posture, RTL support, App Router compatibility, Expo support
- [x] Library chosen with rationale documented
- [x] RTL/Arabic considerations documented
- [x] CVE-safe version pinning policy defined
- [x] Reversal conditions specified
- [ ] Security-auditor agent reviews CVE posture of chosen libraries — see sub-issue DEV-347
issues.title: ADR-004: i18n/l10n Library Standard — next-intl (web) + react-i18next/expo-localization (mobile)

## React Native Mobile App Shell — Android Attendance App

**Architecture reference**: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) (ADR + offline sync + push notification design)
**Prior work**: [DEV-33](mention://issue/f76d10de-667b-48f8-ae1b-d3baa473fee5) scaffold + [DEV-43](mention://issue/585eee66-2638-4dc8-8a61-6f6dab977c07) demo APK (already built)

## Input
- Stack: Expo SDK 50+ (React Native), TypeScript, NativeWind (Tailwind for RN), Zustand (state)
- Target: Android API 24+ (primary); iOS excluded from MVP
- Language: Arabic RTL primary
- Modules: Camera (selfie), Location (GPS), Local SQLite (offline queue), Firebase FCM (push)

## Expected Output
A production-ready Expo app shell with the following screens wired to Supabase:

### Screens
- **Login** (`/`) — Email + password, Arabic RTL, Supabase Auth, stores session in `expo-secure-store`
- **Home / Dashboard** (`/home`) — Today's punch status, shift info, pending leave count
- **Punch Screen** (`/punch`) — "Check In" / "Check Out" button; captures GPS + selfie; calls `/functions/v1/punch`; queues to SQLite if offline
- **Punch History** (`/history`) — List of own punch events (paginated, PostgREST)
- **Leave Request** (`/leave`) — Form: type, dates, reason; submits to `leave_requests` table
- **Leave Status** (`/leave/status`) — Employee's own leave requests with approval status
- **Offline Sync Indicator** — Badge on home screen showing pending offline queue count

### Technical Requirements
- Offline queue: SQLite table `offline_queue` (schema from DEV-30 offline sync design)
- Auto-sync: drain offline queue when network connectivity restored (`expo-network` listener)
- FCM: register device token on login, store in `push_subscriptions` table
- Selfie: `expo-camera` capture → upload to Supabase Storage private bucket
- GPS: `expo-location` foreground permission → lat/lng sent with each punch
- `FCM_ENABLED` env flag (graceful no-op when false, matching DEV-43 pattern)
- Deep link support: `attendance://` scheme for push notification taps

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npx expo start` launches without errors
- [ ] Login screen renders in Arabic RTL (`dir="rtl"` equivalent via NativeWind)
- [ ] Successful login stores session in `expo-secure-store`
- [ ] Punch screen captures GPS and selfie before calling API
- [ ] Offline queue stores punch event when network is unavailable
- [ ] Offline queue drains automatically when connectivity restores
- [ ] FCM token registered on login (or no-op if `FCM_ENABLED=false`)
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode passes (`tsc --noEmit`)

## Dependencies
- Auth sub-issue (Supabase auth utils / `lib/supabase.ts`)
- API sub-issue (Edge Functions must be deployed for punch to work)
- DB schema sub-issue (offline SQLite schema matches server schema)

## Risk
- Camera + Location require Android permissions declared in `app.json` / `AndroidManifest.xml`
- `expo-secure-store` not available on Android emulators older than API 23 — target API 24+ only
- FCM requires `google-services.json` — use `FCM_ENABLED=false` until credentials available

## Definition of Done
- All 7 screens render without crash on Android emulator (API 34)
- Offline queue → sync flow verified manually
- TypeScript strict passes
- Screenshot of login + punch screen attached to comment
- Status flipped to `in_review`

metadata.blocked_reason: DoD not met: emulator test not run, offline sync not manually verified, login+punch screenshots not attached. See QGM verdict comment.

## Supabase Auth Integration — Android Attendance App

**Architecture reference**: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) (comment #3 — RLS Policies + Auth Model)

## Input
From DEV-30 auth model:
- Supabase Auth (email + password; no OAuth for MVP)
- Custom JWT claims injected via Auth Hook: `tenant_id`, `employee_id`, `role`, `department_id`
- Roles: `super_admin`, `hr_manager`, `dept_manager`, `employee`
- JWT payload structure:
  ```json
  {
    "sub": "<auth.users.id>",
    "app_metadata": {
      "tenant_id": "<uuid>",
      "employee_id": "<uuid>",
      "role": "hr_manager",
      "department_id": "<uuid | null>"
    }
  }
  ```
- Session management: 1-hour access token, 7-day refresh token
- Mobile: refresh token stored in `expo-secure-store` (encrypted keychain)
- Web: refresh token handled by Supabase JS SSR client (`@supabase/ssr`)

## Expected Output
1. **Auth Hook function** (`custom_access_token_hook`) — Postgres function that looks up the employee record by `auth.users.id` and injects `tenant_id`, `employee_id`, `role`, `department_id` into JWT claims
2. **Auth Hook registered** in Supabase project hooks configuration (`supabase/config.toml` or dashboard)
3. **Helper functions** in Postgres:
   - `auth_tenant_id()` → `(auth.jwt()->'app_metadata'->>'tenant_id')::uuid`
   - `auth_employee_id()` → `(auth.jwt()->'app_metadata'->>'employee_id')::uuid`
   - `auth_role()` → `auth.jwt()->'app_metadata'->>'role'`
4. **Seed data**: at least one `super_admin` user per tenant for testing (`super_admin@diwan.demo / Demo2026!`)
5. **Auth middleware** for Next.js web: `middleware.ts` using `@supabase/ssr` to refresh sessions on every request
6. **Mobile auth utils**: `lib/supabase.ts` for Expo using `expo-secure-store` as storage adapter

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auth Hook function exists in DB and is registered (`SELECT * FROM auth.hooks`)
- [ ] Login with `super_admin@diwan.demo / Demo2026!` returns JWT with correct `app_metadata` claims
- [ ] JWT decoded client-side shows `role=super_admin`, `tenant_id`, `employee_id` populated
- [ ] `auth_tenant_id()` returns correct UUID when called with an authenticated session
- [ ] Next.js `middleware.ts` refreshes expired access tokens automatically
- [ ] Expo `lib/supabase.ts` uses `expo-secure-store` storage adapter (not AsyncStorage)
- [ ] Seed script creates demo users without committing passwords to repo (env var or vault)

## Dependencies
- DB Schema sub-issue (employees table must exist for hook to query)
- DEV-30 architecture artifacts (auth model comment)

## Risk
- Auth hook runs as SECURITY DEFINER — validate input to prevent privilege escalation
- Seed passwords must NOT be committed to repo — use Supabase CLI secrets or `.env`

## Definition of Done
- Auth hook deployed and tested
- Login flow verified end-to-end with correct JWT claims
- Seed user created and documented
- Status flipped to `in_review`

metadata.blocked_reason: Security review failed: SECURITY DEFINER functions missing SET search_path=''. All 6 functions need search_path='' and public.employees fully qualified in hook. Also: DATABASE_URL not validated in seed-bootstrap.sh. See QGM verdict comment for required fixes.

issues.title: BUILD: Supabase Auth integration — JWT custom claims hook + session management — Android Attendance

## Rationale
The devosnt App Factory has been operational since 2026-05-25 (5+ weeks). As of 2026-07-01, APP — Android Attendance is the only in-flight app and the hub project has 0 build issues. ADR-001, ADR-002, and ADR-003 are all adopted, and the architecture for Android Attendance was completed (schema, API contract). The build has never started because build sub-issues were never created. This means the factory has produced no shipped apps — the compounding value of the factory is zero.

ADR-002 admin blockers prevent the full App Hub model, but the build itself (schema implementation, API routes, frontend shell, auth) can proceed in parallel using the 05 - Parallel Build project, independent of the codegraph layer.

## Evidence
- APP — Android Attendance project (09e6cfd3): status=planned, issue_count=0
- ADR-001: Next.js + Supabase + Vercel adopted (done)
- ADR-003: Expo + EAS Build adopted (in_review, confirms mobile stack)
- DEV-30, DEV-43: Android Attendance earlier issues (check for existing schema/API artifacts)
- Workspace has 04 - Architecture Database API (3 issues, all done), 05 - Parallel Build (8 issues, 6 done)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read DEV-30 and DEV-43 to inventory existing architecture artifacts (schema, API contract, PRD) for Android Attendance
- [ ] Create parallel build sub-issues under APP — Android Attendance project (not Factory Brain) for at minimum: DB schema implementation, API routes (CRUD), Next.js frontend shell, Supabase Auth integration
- [ ] Assign each sub-issue to the appropriate build squad agent
- [ ] Set sub-issues to status=todo for parallel stream start

## Definition of Done
- At least 4 build sub-issues created under the Android Attendance app hub project
- Each sub-issue has: description referencing the architecture artifacts, assignee, priority=medium, status=todo
- Parent APP project lifecycle_phase metadata updated to "build"

## Effort
M

## Next.js Admin Web Frontend Shell — Android Attendance App

**Architecture reference**: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) (ADR comment #1 — stack confirmed; PRD in DEV-26)

## Input
- Stack: Next.js 14 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + Supabase JS client + shadcn/ui
- Target users: Super Admin, HR Manager, Dept Manager (web dashboard only; employees use mobile)
- Language: Arabic RTL primary; English secondary
- Design: RTL-first layout, Kuwait timezone display, Arabic numerals option

## Expected Output
A scaffolded Next.js web admin app with:

### Pages / Routes
- `/login` — Supabase Auth login form (email + password), RTL layout
- `/dashboard` — Summary cards: total check-ins today, late arrivals, pending leaves, active employees
- `/employees` — Employee list with search/filter; CRUD modals (Super Admin / HR Manager only)
- `/offices` — Office list; add/edit office with GPS coordinates + radius (meters)
- `/shifts` — Shift templates CRUD
- `/attendance` — Daily/weekly punch log table; filter by department, date, employee
- `/leaves` — Leave request list; approve/reject actions (HR Manager / Dept Manager)
- `/reports` — Daily + monthly report download (calls `/functions/v1/report-daily` + `/report-monthly`)

issues.description:
## Rationale
Every app built in this factory requires bilingual output (Arabic Kuwaiti + English) per the workspace Language Rules. Yet there is no ADR documenting which i18n/l10n library to use. In June 2026, two critical CVEs landed in the most popular Next.js i18n library ecosystem:
- GHSA-2933-q333-qg83: `i18next-fs-backend` — prototype pollution via crafted missing-key strings (critical)
- GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w: `i18next-http-middleware` — MissingKeyHandler injection (critical)

Without a documented i18n library choice, future build agents may adopt i18next (a common default) without pinning safe versions, exposing apps to these CVEs.

## Evidence
- Workspace Language Rules: "Developer docs: English; bilingual when external party reads. NewTech business/customer/marketing outputs: Arabic Kuwaiti style."
- GHSA-2933-q333-qg83 (critical): i18next-fs-backend prototype pollution
- GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w (critical): i18next-http-middleware injection
- No existing ADR covers i18n strategy
- next-intl (maintained by Vercel community), next-i18next, and react-intl are common alternatives

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evaluate the top 3 Next.js i18n libraries (next-intl, next-i18next, react-intl) against: CVE posture, RTL support, App Router compatibility, Expo support for mobile
- [ ] Choose one library (or two: one for web, one for mobile)
- [ ] Document as ADR-004 with: decision, rationale, RTL/Arabic considerations, safe pinning policy, reversal conditions
- [ ] Create DEV issue for ADR-004, status=in_review once written

## Definition of Done
- ADR-004 issue created in 04 - Architecture Database API project
- ADR body includes: decision, alternatives, RTL support notes, CVE-safe version pinning, reversal conditions
- Security-auditor has reviewed the chosen library's current CVE posture

## Effort
S

## Input
3 blocked issues carry retry_log metadata signatures indicating agent stall:
- DEV-319: retry_log = {"attempts":3,"last_stall":"2026-06-26T07:36:00Z","blocked_at":"2026-06-26T09:00:00Z"}
- DEV-264: retry_log = {"attempts":2,"last_retry":"2026-06-15T07:49:02Z","reason":"stall >90min no comments"}
- DEV-257: retry_log = 3

The CEO routing prompt (agent-self-healing-policy) retries stalled runs but there is no rule in the CEO prompt that:
(a) treats `retry_log` metadata on a blocked issue as a routing failure signal requiring CEO re-assignment
(b) escalates to a different runtime/model after 2 failures (agent-self-healing-policy exists but is not wired into CEO's in_review sweep)

## Expected Output
A targeted patch to the CEO agent's routing prompt adding:
1. A "Retry-log sweep" step in the CEO's weekly/daily loop: for any blocked issue with `retry_log` present and attempts ≥ 2, invoke `agent-self-healing-policy` and re-assign to a different runtime
2. A condition in the in_review sweep: if a blocked issue has `retry_log.attempts >= 3`, escalate to workspace owner via external-blocker-escalation skill instead of re-queuing
3. Clear language distinguishing: (a) a stalled run = re-assign with different runtime, (b) an external credential gap = escalate via external-blocker-escalation, (c) a logic/QA failure = send back to original assignee with failure summary

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CEO prompt patch drafted as a diff showing old vs new routing rules
- [ ] New rule: retry_log.attempts >= 2 → trigger agent-self-healing-policy
- [ ] New rule: retry_log.attempts >= 3 → trigger external-blocker-escalation
- [ ] Distinction documented for: stall vs credential vs logic failure routing paths
- [ ] Patch applied to CEO agent definition via `multica agent update` or equivalent
- [ ] Test: verify DEV-319, DEV-264, DEV-257 would have been caught by the new rule

## Dependencies
- CEO agent ID: d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3
- agent-self-healing-policy skill (already assigned to ceo)
- external-blocker-escalation skill (already in workspace)

## Risk
- CEO prompt changes affect all routing decisions — must be additive rules only, no removal of existing logic
- Test against a non-DEV-36 blocked issue before applying to avoid unintended status changes

## Definition of Done
- CEO prompt patch applied
- Routing rules for stall/credential/logic failure are distinct and documented
- DEV-319, DEV-264, DEV-257 used as test cases in the patch verification
- Patch summary posted as comment on DEV-324

## API Routes Implementation — Android Attendance App

**Architecture reference**: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) (comment #4 — full API contract)

## Input
Complete API contract from DEV-30 including:
- **Standard CRUD via PostgREST** (auto-handled by Supabase): `/rest/v1/employees`, `/rest/v1/offices`, `/rest/v1/shifts`, `/rest/v1/employee_shifts`, `/rest/v1/leave_requests`, `/rest/v1/audit_log`, `/rest/v1/push_subscriptions`
- **Custom Edge Functions** requiring business logic:
  - `POST /functions/v1/punch` — geofence check, rate limit (1/30s per employee), selfie upload, offline queue drain
  - `POST /functions/v1/punch-sync` — batch offline queue drain
  - `POST /functions/v1/leave-approve` — leave approval with audit trail
  - `GET /functions/v1/report-daily` — daily attendance summary
  - `GET /functions/v1/report-monthly` — monthly attendance report (CSV export)
  - `POST /functions/v1/notify-fcm` — FCM push trigger (shift reminder, missed punch, leave decision)
- **Realtime subscriptions**: `punch_events`, `leave_requests` channels (documented; no custom code needed)

## Expected Output
1. Supabase Edge Functions (Deno TypeScript) for all 6 custom endpoints
2. Each function validates JWT claims (tenant isolation), applies business logic, returns typed responses
3. Rate limiting on `/punch` endpoint (1 call per 30 seconds per employee_id)
4. Geofence validation on `/punch` (GPS coordinates vs. office radius from `offices` table)
5. Selfie storage: upload to Supabase Storage private bucket, return signed URL with 90-day TTL
6. `/punch-sync` accepts array of offline queue items, deduplicates by `client_punch_id`
7. OpenAPI 3.1 `openapi.yaml` committed to repo covering all endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 Edge Functions deploy without errors (`supabase functions deploy`)
- [ ] `/punch` rejects double-punch within 30 seconds (rate limit test)
- [ ] `/punch` with GPS outside office radius returns `403 OUTSIDE_GEOFENCE`
- [ ] `/punch-sync` deduplicates by `client_punch_id` (idempotent)
- [ ] `/leave-approve` creates audit_log entry on approval/rejection
- [ ] `/report-daily` returns correct JSON for authenticated HR Manager JWT
- [ ] `openapi.yaml` validates against OpenAPI 3.1 spec

## Dependencies
- [DB Schema sub-issue] must be `in_review` or `done` first (tables must exist)
- DEV-30 (architecture — done)

## Risk
- Edge Functions run in Deno (not Node) — use only Deno-compatible imports
- Geofence logic: use Haversine formula; keep within Edge Function (no external service)

## Definition of Done
- All 6 functions deployed and smoke-tested
- `openapi.yaml` committed
- Status flipped to `in_review`

- [ ] Skill posts a one-line close comment before transitioning to done

- [ ] Skill registered in the workspace and attached to at least workspace-operations-manager agent

## Risk
- Must never close an issue with open sub-issues or unresolved blockers
- Must never close DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog)

- Skill registered and attached to workspace-operations-manager

## Emulator QA — DEV-352: React Native Expo app shell

Parent: [DEV-352](mention://issue/16c91ac2-f0b9-4e88-aa70-b4408bdea1d6)

The build is code-complete (TypeScript strict passes, security reviewed as strong). Three DoD items are unverified and blocking `done`.

## Required tests

### 1. Full emulator smoke test (Android API 34)

Run `npx expo run:android` on an API 34 emulator and confirm all 7 screens launch and render without crash:
- Login (`/`) — Arabic RTL
- Home / Dashboard (`/home`) — punch status, shift info, offline badge
- Punch Screen (`/punch`) — GPS + selfie flow
- Punch History (`/history`) — paginated list
- Leave Request (`/leave`) — form
- Leave Status (`/leave/status`) — approval badges
- Offline Sync Indicator — badge on home screen

### 2. Offline sync flow test

1. Put emulator in airplane mode.
2. Perform a punch on the Punch screen — confirm it shows as queued (not sent).
3. Check SQLite `offline_queue` table has the pending entry.
4. Restore connectivity.
5. Confirm the queue drains automatically and the punch appears in Punch History.

### 3. Screenshots

Capture and attach to a comment on [DEV-352](mention://issue/16c91ac2-f0b9-4e88-aa70-b4408bdea1d6):
- Login screen (must show Arabic RTL text)
- Punch screen (GPS + selfie UI visible)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 screens load without crash on API 34 emulator
- [ ] Offline punch queues to SQLite when airplane mode active
- [ ] Queue drains when connectivity restores; punch appears in history
- [ ] Login + Punch screenshots attached to DEV-352 comment

## On completion

Post a summary comment on [DEV-352](mention://issue/16c91ac2-f0b9-4e88-aa70-b4408bdea1d6) with test results and screenshots attached, then run:

```
multica issue status 16c91ac2-f0b9-4e88-aa70-b4408bdea1d6 in_review
multica issue metadata delete 16c91ac2-f0b9-4e88-aa70-b4408bdea1d6 --key blocked_reason
```

metadata.blocked_reason: Android SDK (adb/emulator) not installed in agent runtime. Cannot run npx expo run:android or capture screenshots. Needs environment with Android SDK 34 + API 34 AVD.

issues.description:
## Blocking: Confirm or add RLS on `punch_rate_limit`

**Raised by**: security-auditor in DEV-348 review.

**Problem**: `punch_rate_limit` has no `tenant_id` column and no documented RLS policy. If `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` is absent, PostgREST exposes all `(employee_id, last_punch_at)` pairs to any authenticated user across all tenants.

```sql
CREATE TABLE punch_rate_limit (
  employee_id    UUID  PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  last_punch_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

## Expected Output

Confirm **one** of the following in a comment on the parent issue DEV-348 and update the migration file accordingly:

**(a)** Add to migration:
```sql
ALTER TABLE punch_rate_limit ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "employee_own_rate_limit" ON punch_rate_limit
  FOR ALL USING (employee_id = auth_employee_id());
```

**(b)** Confirm the table is excluded from PostgREST by revoking SELECT from `anon`/`authenticated` (table is only accessed by Edge Functions via service role):
```sql
REVOKE SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON punch_rate_limit FROM anon, authenticated;
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration file updated with chosen fix (a or b)
- [ ] Confirmed that service role bypasses RLS regardless (expected behavior)
- [ ] Comment posted on DEV-348 with the chosen approach

## Dependencies
- DEV-348 (parent — stays in_review until this closes)

## Definition of Done
- Migration updated with explicit RLS or explicit PostgREST exclusion
- DEV-348 security-auditor confirms the fix clears the blocker
issues.title: FIX: punch_rate_limit RLS — confirm tenant isolation (blocker for DEV-348)

## DB Schema Implementation — Android Attendance App

**Architecture reference**: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) (comment #2 — full SQL schema posted)

## Input
Complete Supabase Postgres schema from DEV-30, including:
- Extensions: `pgcrypto`, `pg_cron`, `btree_gist`
- 11 tables: `tenants`, `departments`, `employees`, `offices`, `shifts`, `employee_shifts`, `punch_events`, `leave_requests`, `audit_log`, `push_subscriptions`, `punch_rate_limit`
- Every table has `tenant_id` FK + `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`
- Civil ID column encrypted with AES-256 via `pgp_sym_encrypt` (key in Supabase Vault)
- `audit_log` append-only trigger on all write tables
- GIST exclusion constraint on `employee_shifts` for shift overlap
- Helper functions: `auth_tenant_id()`, `auth_employee_id()`, `auth_role()`
- Auth hook function: `custom_access_token_hook` (injects role, tenant_id, employee_id into JWT)

## Expected Output
1. Supabase migration file(s) applying the full schema (`.sql` or Supabase CLI `supabase/migrations/`)
2. All 11 tables created with correct columns, types, constraints, foreign keys, indexes
3. RLS enabled on every table
4. `pgcrypto`, `pg_cron`, `btree_gist` extensions registered
5. Audit trigger deployed on all write tables
6. Auth hook function registered

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 11 tables exist in Supabase with correct schema
- [ ] RLS enabled on every table (verify with `SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'`)
- [ ] Civil ID column is `TEXT` type (AES-256 via `pgp_sym_encrypt`, key in Supabase Vault — not hardcoded)
- [ ] `employee_shifts` has GIST exclusion constraint for overlap prevention
- [ ] `custom_access_token_hook` function exists and is registered as Auth hook
- [ ] `audit_log` trigger fires on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE for all write tables
- [ ] Migration file committed to repo

## Dependencies
- DEV-30 (architecture — done, artifacts available in comments)
- Supabase project credentials (use self-hosted demo instance if prod not yet provisioned)

## Risk
- Supabase project may not be provisioned yet → use `self-hosted-supabase-tunnel` skill to spin up local instance
- Civil ID encryption key must be stored as Supabase secret, not hardcoded
- `pg_net` extension required for `notify_leave_decision` trigger + pg_cron HTTP jobs (pre-installed on Supabase Cloud; must be explicit on self-hosted)
- Auth hook requires manual registration in Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Hooks → Custom Access Token (cannot be automated via SQL migration)
- `app.edge_base_url` and `app.service_key` must be set via `ALTER DATABASE` before pg_cron jobs will run

## Definition of Done
- Migration SQL applied successfully
- Schema screenshot or `\dt` output attached as comment
- Status flipped to `in_review`
issues.title: BUILD: Implement Postgres schema + RLS + audit trigger — Android Attendance

## Rationale
DEV-197 (ARCH ACTION: Re-escalate ADR-002 App Hub Model) has been blocked since 2026-06-01 (30+ days) waiting on three items from the workspace owner: (1) GitHub PAT with `repo:create` scope on newtechkw/adminnewtech, (2) Codegraph / Understand-Anything install, (3) Factory Pulse v3 approval. The June Monthly Arch Review dispatched the re-escalation but the blocker remains unresolved as of 2026-07-01. Per workspace operating rules, a dependency that has not moved in 30+ days must be escalated to the workspace owner directly.

## Evidence
- DEV-37 (ADR-002): adopted 2026-05-25, still in_review; APP — Android Attendance hub has 0 issues
- DEV-197: blocked, metadata `waiting_on=admin@newtechkw.com: respond to DEV-37 comment 0316aefd`
- DEV-196 (June Arch Review): ADR-002 rated "contradicted" — same verdict two months running

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Post a formal escalation comment on DEV-197 and DEV-37 addressed to the workspace owner listing the 3 blockers and requesting action within 5 business days
- [ ] If no response within 5 days, document a fallback: simplified App Hub without codegraph (just project + repo, no code-graph layer) and update ADR-002 accordingly

## Definition of Done

S (write comments + conditional ADR patch)

issues.description:
Monthly Architecture & Tool-stack Review — keeps ADRs current, default tech stack honest, and per-integration posture fresh. Runs on the 1st of each month at 11:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3) — Multica's cron parser does not support "nth weekday of month", so day-1 was chosen over "every Sunday + self-gate" to avoid spawning 3 auto-cancelled issues per month. Mode=create_issue (project: 00 - Factory Brain, id 1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052).

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do NOT create additional issues except the up-to-5 architecture-action sub-issues in step (7).
- NEVER mention the CEO (d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3) in any comment — assignment alone is the trigger.
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up or thank-you. Only mention an agent on dispatch sub-issues, and only if assignment alone is insufficient.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state.

REVIEW WINDOW:
- `since = first day of previous calendar month, 00:00 Asia/Kuwait` → `now`. Use RFC3339 UTC for any CLI/API filter.

INPUTS TO PULL (continue on per-source failure — log "skipped <source>: <reason>" to stdout):
- All ADRs in `00 - Factory Brain` (title prefix "ADR-" or label "adr"). `multica issue list --project 1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052 --output json`.
- All apps shipped or in-flight in the window (projects whose titles start with "APP —").
- Default stack ground-truth: scan `multica skill list --output json` plus the workspace CLAUDE.md content surfaced in your runtime brief.
- Per-runtime cost / quota notes: latest "Workspace Upgrade Weekly Loop" issue + comments for the trailing month (project 00 - Factory Brain).
- Per-integration health: upstream release notes via `gh` CLI / public RSS / vendor changelogs. Never use `curl`/`wget` against Multica.
- Open CVEs: GitHub Security Advisories (`gh api /advisories?ecosystem=...`) for Node, Go, Postgres, Next.js, Expo, plus declared SDKs.

STEPS:

(1) ADR audit
   - List every ADR-* issue (any status).
   - For each, classify: still-load-bearing | superseded (cite successor) | contradicted (cite the shipped app / decision that diverged) | stale.
   - Cross-reference each shipped app's PRD / schema / API contract to detect contradictions.
   - Output as a table.

(2) Default tech stack
   - Restate the current default stack per layer (frontend, backend, DB, auth, deploy, mobile, payments, messaging, analytics).
   - For each layer: (a) what matured in the past 30 days (releases, GA milestones), (b) any alternative now worth a re-look, (c) recommendation: hold | watch | re-evaluate.

(3) Per-runtime delta
   - Runtimes in scope: Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Openclaw, Hermes.
   - For each: pricing / quota changes, new capabilities, deprecations, observed failure rate this month (cite any `agent-self-healing-policy` retry_log surfaced on issues).

(4) Per-integration delta
   - Integrations: Supabase, Vercel, KNet, MyFatoorah, Meta (FB/IG/WhatsApp Graph), Google (Maps/OAuth/Workspace), Expo.
   - For each: pricing changes, breaking API changes, new quotas / rate limits, deprecation notices. One bullet per integration. If nothing landed, write "no changes".

(5) Security posture
   - New CVE classes affecting our stack.
   - RLS / secret-handling gaps surfaced this month (cross-reference any `security-review-checklist` failures in `06 - QA Security Performance`).
   - One-paragraph posture summary + bulleted gaps.

(6) Top 5 architecture changes to consider
   - Derive from steps (1)–(5). Rank by impact × urgency. For each: title, rationale (1–2 lines), owner (squad / agent), effort (S/M/L), risk if skipped.
   - List fewer than 5 if fewer are warranted — do NOT invent fillers.
   - If the month is genuinely quiet, list 0 actions and post the comment marker "no changes — green".

(7) Auto-dispatch the Top 5 (or fewer) as sub-issues
   - For each action, create a sub-issue:
     - Title: "ARCH ACTION: <one-line>"
     - Parent: this autopilot's issue id
     - Project: 00 - Factory Brain (1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052)
     - Priority: high for security / CVE; medium for stack or runtime drift; low for "watch" items
     - Status: todo
     - Assignee: solution-architect (77da9a0d-924e-4087-9278-59ba1182b599) by default; database items → database-architect (89c4ed8b-e38b-48c4-9029-19fd7007a255); API contract items → api-architect (614bc86d-6ef1-4f1e-8b3c-b393b43f46d0); integration items → integration-architect (4d62192f-7508-4ed2-99f5-db8b3dabbd9c); security items → security-auditor (17759d29-f9bb-4c4c-910c-b4476723db87).
     - Description includes: rationale, evidence (links to ADR / app / CVE / release notes), Acceptance Criteria, DoD, suggested effort.
   - If the month is "green" (0 actions), skip dispatch but still complete steps (8)–(10).

(8) Post the audit report as ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue. Use this exact shape (HEREDOC via `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin`):

```
# Monthly Architecture & Tool-stack Review — <YYYY-MM>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (Asia/Kuwait)
**Apps reviewed:** <N> shipped, <N> in-flight
**ADRs reviewed:** <N>

## ADR audit
| ADR | Verdict | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ADR-XXX <title> | still-load-bearing \| superseded \| contradicted \| stale | <link> |

## Default tech stack
- Frontend: hold|watch|re-evaluate — <one-line reason>
- Backend: …
- DB: …
- Auth: …
- Deploy: …
- Mobile: …
- Payments: …
- Messaging: …
- Analytics: …

## Per-runtime
- Claude Code: <delta or "no changes">
- Codex: …
- Opencode: …
- Openclaw: …
- Hermes: …

## Per-integration
- Supabase: …
- Vercel: …
- KNet: …
- MyFatoorah: …
- Meta: …
- Google: …
- Expo: …

## Security posture
<one paragraph + bulleted gaps, or "no new CVE classes; no RLS/secret gaps surfaced this month">

## Top 5 architecture changes
1. **<title>** — owner: <agent/squad> | effort: S/M/L | risk-if-skipped: <one line>
2. …

(If the month is quiet: "no changes — green")

## Dispatched sub-issues
- DEV-NN — <ARCH ACTION title>
- …
```

Do NOT include any `mention://agent/...` link in this comment.

(9) Pin metadata on this issue ONLY when materially useful for future runs. Recommended keys:
   - `review_window_end` (RFC3339)
   - `adrs_reviewed` (number)
   - `apps_reviewed` (number)
   - `actions_dispatched` (number, 0–5)
   - `posture` (string: green | yellow | red)
   Use `multica issue metadata set` once each; skip any key whose value matches what `metadata list` already shows.

(10) Flip this issue's status to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). Do NOT close it — the CEO Command Squad confirms Architecture Squad has actioned the dispatched sub-issues. Sub-issues run on their own assignees.

OUTPUT STDOUT (one line only, no extra comment):
`Monthly Arch Review @ <RFC3339> | adrs=N | apps=N | actions=N | posture=<green|yellow|red>`

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI / `gh` / HTTP failure. Log "skipped <source>: <reason>" and continue.
- Even an empty month gets a "no changes — green" comment — never silent.
- Cap dispatch at 5 sub-issues hard.
- Read metadata before pinning. Only update keys that changed since last review.
- All comments go on the auto-created issue or on the dispatch sub-issues this run spawned. Never comment on DEV-36 or any unrelated issue.

---

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.

    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.

    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

issues.description:
## SECURITY REVIEW: NotFair skill import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** DEV-337 (SKILL IMPORT: NotFair)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/nowork-studio/NotFair
**License:** MIT
**Language:** TypeScript
**Install footprint:** `npm install -g @notfair/cli` + Claude Code plugin via marketplace

### What to review

**1. OAuth scopes (high risk)**
NotFair connects to Google Ads, Meta Ads (Facebook/Instagram), and Google Search Console via OAuth. Review:
- Which OAuth scopes are requested for Google Ads — does it request read-only or read+write?
- Which scopes for Meta Ads API?
- Does it ever store tokens beyond the session?
- Does the MCP server expose OAuth token material over MCP tool calls?

**2. External data egress (medium risk)**
NotFair has a `.mcp.json` and a companion SaaS at `notfair.co`. Review:
- Does the MCP server call `notfair.co` or any external endpoint with user account data?
- Does the plugin phone home for analytics or telemetry?
- What data (account IDs, campaign metrics, search queries) leaves the user's environment?

**3. Dependency audit (medium risk)**
- Run `npm audit` on the package
- Check for known CVEs in key deps
- Verify no supply-chain indicators (postinstall scripts, unexpected network calls)

**4. Credential handling (high risk)**
- Verify OAuth tokens are not logged to stdout/stderr
- Verify tokens are not written to any plain-text files
- Check `.gitignore` to confirm credentials are excluded

### Decision Criteria
- **green**: OAuth is read-only + minimum-privilege, no external egress of account data to third parties, no credential leakage in logs
- **red**: Any of the following: write-scope OAuth, account data sent to third-party servers without user consent, credentials logged or stored insecurely

### When done
Set `decision=green` or `decision=red` in this issue's metadata.
If green: promote DEV-337 to `todo` (`multica issue status 54c9c270-2af8-4f45-9d2c-0e53a9b121e4 todo`).
If red: set `blocked_reason=security_review_failed` on DEV-337 and leave it backlog.

### Definition of Done
- All 4 review areas above checked and documented in a comment
- `decision` metadata key set on this issue
- DEV-337 either promoted (green) or blocked with reason (red)

issues.description:
## BENCHMARK ACTION: EmbeddedSecurityScanning

**Source sweep:** DEV-334 (2026-06-29)
**Pattern origin:** Lovable shipped Aikido Security penetration testing on ALL plans (including Free) on June 22, 2026 — the first vendor in the six-vendor cohort to offer automated security scanning at zero cost, raising the security floor for all builders and positioning "security by default" as a marketing differentiator. Pattern name: **EmbeddedSecurityScanning**.

## Input

- **Pattern observed:** Automated security scanning (Aikido pen-testing) integrated into the build platform and available on every plan tier, including Free. Lovable ships this as a no-configuration feature — builders get it without setting anything up. Source: https://docs.lovable.dev/changelog (entry: "Aikido penetration testing is now available on all plans," June 22, 2026).
- **Vendors shipping it:** Lovable (1/6 explicitly; closest adjacent is our existing `security-review` checklist skill).
- **Why it matters:** Lovable is the first in the cohort to automate security scanning at the platform layer, not just the advice layer. This narrows the gap between Lovable's build-and-deploy pipeline and our App Factory QA Gate. Our existing `security-review` skill is checklist-driven and agent-run; an automated scanner would move this from "agent-executed checklist" to "always-on scan with structured output."

## Expected Output

An updated or new skill that formally integrates a security scanner as a mandatory phase in the App Factory Build → QA Gate transition:

1. **Primary:** New skill `embedded-security-scan` (or update to existing `security-review`) that:
   - Defines the integration contract for Aikido API (or equivalent: Snyk, Semgrep, or Trivy for open-source alternative)
   - Runs scan as a structured step and emits results as an issue comment in a standard format
   - Includes graceful fallback to the existing checklist when no scanner credentials are configured (fallback must be the default, not the edge case)
2. **Secondary (if API credentials are not yet available):** Publish the skill spec with the integration point fully documented and the fallback mode confirmed working — so any build agent can activate full scanning when credentials arrive via `multica agent custom_env set`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Skill published in Project 08 - Skill Library and discoverable via `multica skill list`
- [ ] Skill trigger is clear: invocable as `/embedded-security-scan` or auto-triggered by the QA Gate skill at the Build→QA transition
- [ ] Skill includes: (a) scanner API call step (spec the Aikido/Snyk/Semgrep integration point), (b) checklist fallback confirmed working without credentials, (c) scan result → issue comment in a standard structured format
- [ ] No credentials committed to skill content — all credentials via `custom_env`
- [ ] At least one end-to-end test run documented (fallback mode acceptable as first run)
- [ ] Skill content passes security-review-checklist (no secrets, no executable injection risk)

## Dependencies

- Existing `security-review` skill (baseline to extend rather than replace; avoid regression)
- Aikido API credentials (or Snyk/Semgrep/Trivy) from admin@newtechkw.com — optional for first delivery; fallback mode must work without them
- Project 08 - Skill Library write access (standard for Skill Factory squad)

## Risk

- **Credential dependency:** If Aikido or another scanner is not provisioned, the skill must gracefully degrade to checklist mode. Do not block delivery on credentials — deliver the spec + fallback first, activate scanner when credentials land.
- **Scope creep:** The skill specifies the integration point; it does NOT need to provision the scanner account or run a full audit of existing projects. First delivery is the skill artifact, not a workspace-wide retroactive scan.
- **Regression:** The existing `security-review` skill must continue to work. If extending it, preserve all existing checklist steps.

## Definition of Done

- Skill published and discoverable in Project 08
- Fallback mode (existing checklist) confirmed working in at least one test run without scanner credentials
- Aikido/scanner integration point fully documented in SKILL.md even if not yet active
- Scan output format defined (structured comment format for downstream QA Gate agents to parse)
- No secrets in any committed skill content
- QA Gate agent briefed or skill updated to invoke this skill at Build→QA transition

(4) Score the cross-vendor pattern set. Aggregate every "pattern to copy" across the six briefs and rank by:

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

Workspace Upgrade Weekly Loop — recursive workspace self-improvement. Mode=create_issue. Runs every Sunday 09:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3).

Run the `workspace-upgrade-report` skill against the live workspace and post a single ranked action list as a comment on the issue this autopilot creates. Auto-dispatch the top 3 actions to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, and memory-curator. Never skip a week — a silent skip is itself a finding.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the three sub-issues for top-3 dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention CEO in any comment — CEO is the assignee and re-mentioning would loop (Factory Pulse v3 no-mention rule).
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up. Only mention agents to delegate first-touch work on a fresh sub-issue.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state. Stay within the issue this autopilot creates.

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

(2) Collect the five input signals:

   (a) retry_logs (last 7 days). Walk every issue updated in the past 7 days and pull metadata. Any `retry_log` key present is a failure signature. Aggregate by skill name / agent / failure_reason.
       - `multica issue list --output json --status in_progress` and `--status blocked` and `--status in_review` (paged if needed).
       - For each issue, `multica issue metadata list <id> --output json` — keep only entries with a `retry_log` key.

   (b) Blocked issues — every issue currently `status=blocked`:
       - `multica issue list --status blocked --output json`
       - For each: capture id, identifier, title, assignee, age (now - updated_at), and `blocked_reason` metadata.

   (c) Stale in_review (>48h) — every issue currently `status=in_review` whose `updated_at` is more than 48h ago:
       - `multica issue list --status in_review --output json`
       - Filter client-side by `updated_at`.

   (d) Agent WIP imbalance — for every agent in the workspace, count `in_progress` issues assigned to them. Flag any agent with WIP=0 (cold) or WIP≥4 (overloaded). Flag any squad whose total WIP ≥7.
       - `multica agent list --output json`
       - `multica issue list --status in_progress --output json` and group by `assignee_id`.

   (e) Skill orphans — every skill not assigned to any agent (`agent_count==0`), and every agent with zero skills attached.
       - `multica skill list --output json`
       - For each skill, attempt `multica skill get <id> --output json` (or inspect the skill→agent join if exposed). If the CLI exposes no join command, infer from agent definitions (`multica agent get <id> --output json` per agent) — cap the scan at 30 agents per run.

(3) Score and rank actions. For each candidate upgrade action, score on:
   - Impact (how many issues/agents this would unblock or accelerate; 1–5)
   - Effort (1=trivial, 5=multi-day)
   - Confidence (1=speculative, 5=clear pattern with ≥3 occurrences)
   Final score = (Impact × Confidence) / Effort. Surface the Top 5.

(4) Compose the upgrade report comment. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue, using this exact shape so step (6) can parse it:

```
# Workspace Upgrade Report — <YYYY-MM-DD>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC)
**Signals scanned:** retry_logs=N | blocked=N | stale_in_review_48h=N | wip_outliers=N | skill_orphans=N

## Top 5 Upgrade Actions

1. **<short title>** — owner=<agent-name> | effort=<S|M|L> | impact=<1-5> | score=<n>
   - Rationale: <one sentence, cite source issue identifiers like DEV-NN>
   - Expected impact: <one sentence>

2. … (same shape)
3. … (same shape)
4. … (same shape)
5. … (same shape)

## Signal Summary
- Retry log hotspots: <skill / agent / count> …
- Stale in_review: <DEV-NN list>
- Blocked: <DEV-NN list>
- WIP outliers: <agent: count> …
```
